fiat deposit provider for Infinex: Bridge.xyz

Bridge.xyz fiat deposit mechanic — rail shape: Bridge virtual accounts are permanent, reusable fiat deposit addresses that convert incoming fiat into crypto and deliver it to a specified destination wallet address. Bridge handles fiat-to-crypto conversion and sends funds on-chain automatically.
Bridge.xyz supported fiat deposit rails: USD (ACH/wire, US account+routing number), EUR (SEPA IBAN), MXN (SPEI CLABE), BRL (PIX BR code), GBP (FPS, beta), COP (Bre-B, beta)
Bridge.xyz virtual account KYC precondition: Customers must be onboarded and KYC/KYB-approved before virtual accounts can be created

Bridge.xyz fiat deposit settlement output: Fiat deposits are converted to a target crypto (e.g. USDC) and delivered on-chain to a configured destination wallet address on a specified payment rail (e.g. Ethereum)

Bridge.xyz virtual account deposit event lifecycle: Events: funds_received → payment_submitted → payment_processed (final, confirmed on-chain). Also: funds_scheduled (ACH in transit), in_review, refunded states.

old_audit.placement_read: Light + Direct + Sustained + Bound — self-contained tempo; the motor is stalled at Gliding, not releasing into Dabbing. Drive read lands as Doing (Weight suppressed, Space+Time+Flow active but Flow is Bound, so the action stays functional-informational). No Spell, no Vision — the weight of changed reality that Infinex requires is missing. The copy is technically correct and movement-clean but sits in a register below the character's assigned placement.

infinex_fit.reason: self-contained is a beat-only / reserve tempo for Infinex, not a primary-allowed tempo. The full available palette includes self-contained as reserve only. More critically: self-contained (Light + Direct + Stable) carries no Weight-stress and no Flow — it cannot activate the Spell drive or the Penetrating aspect that define Infinex's placement. The through-action is 'to reveal that the bank wall just dissolved inside one account' — that dissolution requires either sombre (Strong + Bound weight of the old wall falling) or irradiant (Light + Free lift of the new possibility opening), or at minimum the sociable (Direct + Free) warmth of invitation. None of those factors are firing here. The copy is informational, not revelatory. Passion/time-pressure drift is not the problem here — the failure is the opposite: the copy has deflated below character, not overheated above it.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: sociable (Remote outer; Direct + Free; motor Gliding -> Dabbing) — nearest structurally because it shares Direct + Light and the same motor chain (Gliding -> Dabbing), but requires Free flow instead of Bound, and a partner-credit spatial orientation. The lining ('the bank wall just stopped existing') wants the weight of sombre or the lift of irradiant, both of which are primary-allowed. Either would be legal and on-character.

voice_issues: The copy deploys the correct facts and omits correctly, but the through-action ('reveal that the bank wall just dissolved') is not performed — the text announces a mechanic rather than revealing a changed condition. The reader is told what to do and what happens, not shown that a wall they didn't know existed has stopped existing.

The simile 'the way you'd send it to a bank' is doing the right conceptual work (familiarity bridge) but is executing in a Light + Bound register that contains the revelation instead of releasing it. The simile becomes a suppressor rather than a setup.

'It lands as USDC on-chain' is the closest the copy comes to a revelation moment, but it is delivered flat (Gliding, not Dabbing) — the arrival weight is not loaded before the landing. There is no prepared Strong press that the Light landing can release against.
notes_for_actor: The motor you need is Gliding → Dabbing, and you have the Gliding. The Dabbing requires Quick — a sudden, light, accurate touch at the arrival point. Right now 'It lands as USDC on-chain' is a second Glide, not a Dab. Sharpen the arrival: the landing word ('on-chain', or whatever carries the revelation) needs to be a clean, quick, light contact — not another smooth continuation.

The through-action is revelation, not instruction. 'Send fiat to Infinex the way you'd send it to a bank' sets up the known world correctly — but you then need to break it, not continue it. The second sentence must register as a different kind of statement than the first. Right now both sentences are in the same tempo. The Lining says: the wall stopped existing. Make sentence two feel like a different room, not the same corridor.

Weight is absent. Self-contained is Light + Direct — that is the correct motor for beat-level informational copy, but Infinex's primary tempi require at least occasional Strong contact (commanding, practical, sombre, warm) or the character has no gravity. In a two-sentence unit, one of the two sentences should land with more force than the other. Currently both are Light. Consider whether the simile clause — 'the way you'd send it to a bank' — is the place to load weight (the old world was heavy; the old wall had weight), so the release into USDC on-chain can be genuinely Light by contrast.

Flow is Bound throughout. The through-action ('reveal that the wall dissolved') requires at least a moment of Free flow — a release, an opening, an overflow of implication beyond the stated fact. One small structural move: allow the second sentence to carry more than it literally says. 'It lands as USDC on-chain' tells the mechanic; it does not release the implication. A Free-flow version of the same fact lets the reader feel the space that has opened, not just the fact that was deposited.

Reserve self-contained for copy where Infinex is being functional and supportive (help text, confirmation messages, secondary callouts). For a release card announcing a structural change to how money enters the product, the character needs to be present at primary-tempo level — commanding, sombre, irradiant, or sociable. Pick one and commit to it. Do not split the difference with a smooth informational glide.

text:
Your bank account and your wallet have always been two separate things.

Inside Infinex, they aren't anymore.

Send USD, EUR, MXN, BRL, GBP, or COP the way you'd send it to a bank. It arrives on-chain.

old_audit.placement_read: Strong + Direct + Quick + Bound. The piece reads as a Doing Drive activation: Weight (Strong), Space (Direct), Time (Quick) all active; Flow (Bound) is the subdued/latent factor. This is institutional force delivered in short aimed bursts — the classic Pressing → Punching motor at sentence two, with a Dabbing close. The character is acting on the world with precision and economy. No dream-layer, no warm relational tissue, no distant-generous architecture. The mapper-who-already-decided is present, but the tone is closer to the banker executing a directive than the trailblazer revealing a dissolved wall.

infinex_fit.reason: commanding tempo (Strong + Direct, motor Pressing → Punching) is a Primary Allowed tempo for Infinex and the working actions are legal. However the Drive read is Doing (Flow subdued/latent), not Spell (Time subdued/latent) or Vision (Weight subdued/latent). The Spell Drive requires Time to recede and Flow to be active; Vision requires Weight to recede. Here Time is Quick and prominent, and Flow is Bound and prominent — both are asserting, not receding. Quick + Bound is the Passion quadrant of Mobile (unacknowledged: Quick + Bound motor Punching/Slashing) which is explicitly off-spec for Infinex. The piece has the right tempo label but the wrong Drive engine underneath it. The 'wall just dissolved' through-action requires the consequence to arrive with some suspension (Time receding) or generous release (Free Flow) — neither is present. The through-action ('reveal that the bank wall just dissolved') calls for a sombre or irradiant register underneath the commanding surface; what arrived is a command with urgency pressure baked into its speed.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: commanding at Spell-Drive depth: keep Strong + Direct (preserve the landing weight and straight-line path), let Time recede (slow the pivot — allow the historical wall sentence to be more sustained before the negation strikes), shift Flow toward Free (let 'they aren't anymore' release rather than close — the wall dissolving should feel like an opening, not a door shutting). Motor: Pressing → Punching remains but the Pressing phase needs more duration so the Punching release reads as inevitable rather than urgent. Alternatively: sombre (Strong + Bound, Adream outer) if the actor wants to lead with the weight of the dissolved wall rather than the strike.

factual_issues: Bridge.xyz fiat deposit code status: the fact receipt confirms Bridge.xyz is not yet in the platform codebase. The copy reads as a current capability ('Send USD... It arrives on-chain') without any future/upcoming qualifier. If the feature is not yet live, this is a factual presentation issue — the prose performs present-tense delivery of a not-yet-deployed function.
voice_issues: The through-action is 'reveal that the bank wall just dissolved' — but the prose performs the wall dissolving as a command announcement rather than a revelation. The difference: a revelation allows the reader to arrive at the discovery; a command tells the reader what has happened. Sentences one and two are pure tell — 'always been separate / aren't anymore' — with no movement that lets the reader experience the dissolution. The obstacle (reader skims a fintech announcement) is partially addressed by omitting Bridge.xyz, but the commanding tempo still reads as an announcement because the Quick + Bound motor closes the piece before the reader can feel the open space where the wall used to be.

The currency list (USD, EUR, MXN, BRL, GBP, COP) performs as a features enumeration even though the fact receipt marks 'supported currencies as a list' as not-the-point. The list is syntactically positioned as the proof of the claim, which re-introduces the rail-framing the lining was trying to dissolve.

'the way you'd send it to a bank' — this phrase does useful cognitive work (maps the familiar action) but 'to a bank' momentarily re-erects the two-world model the opening sentence was dismantling. The wall is named again just before the piece closes.
notes_for_actor: TIMING CORRECTION — let the Pressing phase breathe before the Punch: Sentence one ('have always been two separate things') is currently Quick-weighted because the pivot to sentence two is too fast. Extend the Pressing duration — the historical wall should feel like it has genuine mass before it's negated. One technique: add a syntactic pause or line break between sentences one and two that forces the reader to hold the weight of 'always' before the negation arrives.

FLOW CORRECTION — open the close: 'Inside Infinex, they aren't anymore.' currently lands Bound — it closes. The through-action is dissolution, which is a Free-Flow event (something releasing, not shutting). Rewrite the negation sentence so it opens rather than terminates. E.g. shift from 'they aren't anymore' (door-closing) toward something that lets the space of the dissolved wall be felt as an opening. The sentence should release, not conclude.

DRIVE CORRECTION — subdue Time, activate Flow: The current Drive is Doing (Weight + Space + Time active, Flow subdued). Infinex at Spell Drive needs Time to recede and Flow to be active. Practically: reduce the Quick motor in the mid-section. The instruction sentence ('Send USD... It arrives on-chain') is operating at Fast transaction speed, which is Passion-adjacent pressure. Slow the arrival — let 'It arrives on-chain' be Sustained rather than Quick so the reader feels the permanence of the new state, not the speed of the transfer.

CURRENCY LIST — remove or reposition: The list is doing features work that undermines the wall-dissolution frame. If the six currencies must appear, move them below the three-sentence nucleus (as a subordinate detail) rather than inside the main body where they perform as a checklist. The nucleus should dissolve the wall; the list can confirm the width of the opening afterward.

BANK ECHO — cut 'to a bank': 'the way you'd send it to a bank' re-introduces the two-world vocabulary mid-close. The reader has just been told the wall is gone; naming 'a bank' as the reference point reinstates the distinction. Replace with a motion-based description of the familiar action (e.g. 'the way you'd send it to anyone') or cut the qualifier entirely and let 'Send USD... It arrives on-chain.' stand as the self-sufficient proof.

old_audit.placement_read: Light + Direct + Quick + Free. The line sits in Stable / Light + Direct (self-contained tempo), with Free flow lifting it briefly toward Floating. No Spell activation visible: there is no Weight-as-gravity, no Bound containment, no subdued Flow tension that Spell requires. The through-action ('the bank wall just dissolved') is not present in the surface. What is present is a clean informational delivery — efficient, cheerful, transactional. Doing Drive (Weight + Space + Time, Flow latent) is the closest drive read, but even that is borderline; the Weight is too Light for strong Doing. The copy reads closer to a product-feature micro-tooltip than a character statement.

infinex_fit.reason: self-contained is a beat-only/reserve tempo for Infinex, not a primary allowed tempo. More critically, the motion evidence does not carry the Spell signature the character requires: there is no Bound Flow containment, no Strong Weight gravity, no sense that decisions have already been taken and the speaker is now mapping from a position of settled inner authority. The line is Light + Free — it floats the fact rather than landing it. The lining ('the bank wall just dissolved') is fully suppressed; the surface is purely informational. The character's super-objective — to take responsibility for the tech so the user only has to want — requires the speaker to carry weight on behalf of the reader. This line carries none; it hands the reader a quick light tap and moves on.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: sociable (Remote outer; Direct + Free; motor Gliding → Dabbing) — this is the closest legal read, and it is beat-only/reserve. The line could be legal as a beat inside a longer sociable passage, but it cannot stand as primary copy without a weight-bearing sentence doing the Spell work nearby.

voice_issues: The lining is fully absent from the surface. The through-action is 'to reveal that the bank wall just dissolved inside one account.' Nothing in the two sentences creates that revelation. The reader receives a feature description, not a dissolution.

The bank analogy ('the way you'd send it to a bank') positions Infinex as bank-equivalent rather than bank-superseding. This reinforces the reader's prior ('fiat comes from banks, crypto lives in wallets') rather than breaking it.

'It converts and lands' attributes effortlessness to a technical process but gives no weight to what that effortlessness means for the reader's world. The character should carry that weight; currently the sentence carries it as a mere feature claim.
notes_for_actor: Add Strong Weight to sentence one. The character has already made the decision; the reader needs to feel the settled mass of that decision in the first sentence, not just an invitation. A single word or construction that lands with gravity — not urgency, not excitement — will shift the motor from Dabbing toward Pressing and activate the Spell axis.

Convert the bank analogy from equivalence to dissolution. 'The way you'd send it to a bank' tells the reader these systems are now equivalent. The lining requires the reader to feel that the wall between them stopped existing. Replace equivalence framing with outcome framing: what the reader now has that they did not have before.

Add Bound Flow to the first sentence, Free Flow to the second. Spell requires Bound containment — a sense that something is being held, that the speaker knows more than they are releasing. Sentence one should feel contained/authoritative; sentence two should release the fact. Currently both sentences are Free, which produces informational float rather than character weight.

Remove or invert the Quick Time signature. 'Converts and lands' reads as fast and easy — correct for the user experience, but it makes the speaker sound like they are selling a convenience feature. The character's tempo primary is commanding or sombre, both Sustained. Consider a construction that holds the duration of the transformation even briefly — a syntactic pause or a held verb — before releasing the USDC landing.

The through-action is dissolution, not delivery. Audit whether the reader, after reading these two sentences, feels that a wall came down or that they received a product update. If it reads as product update, the lining is not executing. The correction is not more technical detail — it is a weight-bearing reframe of what the reader's world looks like after this is true.

old_audit.placement_read: Strong + Direct + Quick + Bound reads as Stable attitude with Punching working action dominant. The piece operates in the Doing Drive (Weight + Space + Time active; Flow subdued but present as Bound containment rather than expressive Adapting). The piece does not exhibit the held relational warmth or slow thaw that would signal Spell. It is institutional declaration: facts hammered into place at speed.

infinex_fit.reason: The prose lands in commanding tempo (Stable; Strong + Direct; motor Pressing -> Punching). Commanding is on the primary allowed palette for Infinex. However, the Time factor is reading Quick, not Sustained. Commanding in the Infinex frame requires Sustained pressure — the weight of a decision already taken, mapped over time, not the staccato of fast assault. The Quick + Bound combination produces a Doing Drive flavour (execution-urgency) rather than the Spell Drive flavour (weight-without-hurry, vision held steady). The piece is firing as Pure Doing — all three active factors at maximum attack rate — which is the Passion-adjacent failure mode (urgency, speed-of-claim) even without explicit FOMO language. Commanding is legal but the Sustained quality that anchors it to Infinex's 'decisions already taken' posture is absent. The piece is too fast.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: commanding — but only recoverable if Time is shifted from Quick to Sustained. Slow the claim. Let it press rather than punch. The same Strong + Direct factors are correct; the Sustained quality is what makes this mapping rather than striking.

factual_issues: GBP (FPS) and COP (Bre-B) are listed in the fact contract as beta rails but the prose writes 'Wire, ACH, SEPA, PIX, SPEI' — GBP FPS and COP Bre-B are omitted, which is defensible given beta status, but SPEI (MXN) is included without any beta qualification. The fact contract does not mark SPEI as beta, so inclusion is clean. No factual error, but actor should confirm SPEI is GA not beta before publish.

The fact contract states 'Bridge.xyz fiat deposit code status in Infinex platform: Not yet in platform codebase'. The prose says 'Your Infinex account now has a deposit account' — present tense, active claim. If the code has not yet landed, this is a prospective announcement written as a present-state fact. This is a material factual risk: the prose claims the feature exists now ('now has') when the deployed fact says code is not in codebase. This needs resolution before release.
voice_issues: Quick tempo is the dominant voice error. The piece reads as a product launch tweet optimised for scroll-stop, not a mapping dispatch from a character who already knows the terrain. Infinex speaks after the decision — the prose should feel like the territory has been surveyed, not like someone is punching news out ahead of competitors.

'The seam between the two stopped existing' is the strongest line and the most character-accurate — it carries the ontological weight of Spell (the wall dissolved, not 'we built a bridge'). The rest of the piece does not earn this line because it arrives too fast, before any sustained weight has been laid. The final line needs more approach, not more setup copy.

The working actions stacking as Punching throughout creates a martial texture inconsistent with Infinex's 'banker who found the better way' image. Infinex does not punch; it presses until the old structure gives way.
notes_for_actor: Slow the Time factor. The current piece is Quick throughout — short sentences completing in under two seconds each, with no held duration. Shift at least two sentences into Sustained by extending the claim arc: let a sentence build before it resolves. This is not about adding words; it is about placing the resolution later in the syntax so the weight has time to press rather than strike.

The preparation is missing. Pressing -> Punching requires the Pressing to happen first. In the current draft the punch lands on sentence one ('Your Infinex account now has a deposit account'). There is no weight-build before the claim. Consider a single sentence of Pressing before the first punch: a line that establishes the old topology (fiat arrives into banks; crypto lives in wallets) so that 'The seam between the two stopped existing' is the consequence of accumulated weight, not an unearned declaration.

Protect 'The seam between the two stopped existing.' This is the Spell Drive moment — it has the held-weight-plus-dissolve quality the character requires. The rest of the piece should serve this line, not compete with it in punching register. Consider making it the final working action after a sustained build, not a line that arrives at the same speed as 'Wire, ACH, SEPA, PIX, SPEI.'

Resolve the present-tense factual tension before release. The prose claims present availability ('now has') against a fact contract that records zero code presence in the platform. If this is a pre-ship announcement, the tense must shift or a qualifier must land — otherwise the piece will fail the Super-Objective validation criterion (real user discovers real product).

The rails list ('Wire, ACH, SEPA, PIX, SPEI') is currently a Punching list — five named strikes. To move this toward Pressing or Gliding (Sustained), consider reframing the list as evidence of breadth rather than a roll-call of features: the point is that money arrives the way it always has, not that five rails have been integrated. The Stable character does not itemise weapons; it describes the terrain.

More critically, the read is Doing-drive (functional reporting) rather than Spell or Vision-drive. The character image is banker-turned-trailblazer who has already decided and is now mapping — that requires penetrating quality, relational warmth, or forward-cast light. Alternatively, sociable (Remote outer; Direct + Free) would keep the Direct path but release Flow — smooth, for-the-reader, partner-credit, no ego — which suits 'to reveal that the bank wall just dissolved inside one account' without overplaying it.

factual_issues: No factual errors detected. The two sentences accurately compress: Bridge virtual accounts receive fiat → convert to USDC → deliver on-chain. The omissions (rails list, KYC, lifecycle) are consistent with the fact receipt justifications.
voice_issues: The through-action is 'to reveal that the bank wall just dissolved inside one account.' The copy does not perform this revelation — it describes a mechanic. 'Fund your Infinex account the way you fund a bank account' is an instruction analogy, not a disclosure of structural change. The reader receives a how-to, not a before/after of their reality.

Flow is Bound where Infinex placement requires Free or at minimum a release gesture. The piece closes on 'on-chain' — a technical confirmation, not an opening. For irradiant or sociable, Flow must open: the sentence needs to hand something forward, not seal itself.

The banker-turned-trailblazer character maps to a territory ('decisions already taken, now mapping'). The mapping quality — generous orientation, showing the reader where they are — is absent. Both sentences are pointing at the product, not at the reader's new situation.
notes_for_actor: The bank-wall dissolution is the event. Write the second sentence as the revelation, not the confirmation. Try: 'It lands as USDC — your bank account and your Infinex account are now the same account.' Let Flow release on that landing rather than seal it with 'on-chain.'

Sentence one reads as instruction ('Fund your account the way you fund a bank account'). Infinex character does not instruct — they map. Recast sentence one as an observation about the reader's new situation: 'Your Infinex account now takes deposits the way your bank account does.' Same fact, reader-oriented, not command-oriented. This moves Space from aimed-at-product to aimed-at-reader.

To reach irradiant: add a warmth-word or a forward-gesture after the USDC landing. Not a rail list — a gesture. 'It lands as USDC, on-chain — already where you need it.' The comma-pause then opens rather than closes.

To reach sociable: keep Direct pathing but release the close. Drop the technical precision of 'on-chain' or let it breathe: 'Fund it the way you fund a bank account. It arrives as USDC.' The period then reads as a clean handoff rather than a sealed report.

Do not add scarcity, urgency, or deadline language to fix the flatness — that is the Passion drift failure mode explicitly flagged for this character. The fix is warmth and release (Flow Free), not pressure (Time Quick + Strong).

old_audit.placement_read: Strong + Direct + Sustained + Bound throughout. The prose reads as Stable-attitude in full Pressing motor — institutionally weighted, single-pathed, sealed. The Drive activation is Doing (Weight + Space + Time, Flow latent/subdued) — the piece has no dreaming overflow, no relational warmth, no vision-lift. It is pure declarative task-completion. The Pressing motor is loaded but never detonates into Punching; the Bound flow prevents release. This is a sealed institutional announcement, not a character delivering from inside the Spell/Vision axis.

infinex_fit.reason: The primary tempo reads as commanding (Stable; Strong + Direct; Pressing motor). Commanding is on the Infinex allowed palette and is therefore not disqualifying in isolation. The legal problem is the Drive read: the piece fires Doing (Weight + Space + Time, Flow subdued), which is the off-axis drive for this character. Infinex placement requires the Spell → Vision axis (Space + Flow + Weight, Time subdued). Flow here is Bound and fully sealed — it is not a Spell or Vision activation but a containment signature. The character's mapping posture (decisions already taken, now mapping) requires Sustained + Flexible + Free in some combination; the Fixed + Direct + Bound configuration here removes all the Flexible and Free evidence the Vision or Spell drives require. The piece also has no Penetrating aspect — there is no sentence that offers the reader a pathway into the space. Every line closes a door rather than opening a chamber. That aspect suppression is the structural problem: the character is Stable + Penetrating + Flow by placement; this prose is Stable + Pressing (no Penetrating, no Flow release). Result: wrong Drive axis, wrong Aspect, wrong working-action outcome.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: commanding — the tempo itself is on-palette, but the Drive and Aspect beneath it are misaligned. If commanding is the chosen tempo, the Actor needs to add Penetrating aspect (sentences that open a pathway, not only close a fact) and release some Flow (not sealing each sentence — one sentence should overflow, or one unit should connect forward without a full-stop cap). That would move the read from Doing toward Spell-in-commanding-expression.

factual_issues: GBP FPS and COP Bre-B rails listed in the fact contract as 'beta' are absent from the copy — correctly omitted. No factual inflation detected.
Bridge.xyz identity (Stripe acquisition) correctly suppressed per the not_said contract. No partnership-framing leak.
KYC precondition correctly omitted. No mechanic bleed.
USDC named as output currency — consistent with 'Bridge.xyz fiat deposit settlement output' deployed fact. Clean.
PIX and SPEI both present in the rail list — consistent with deployed facts. Clean.

voice_issues: The through-action is 'to reveal that the bank wall just dissolved inside one account.' The prose attempts this but seals the revelation inside Bound flow — the wall dissolves, but the sentence reporting it is itself a wall. The character should feel the dissolving, not just report it.

The final line 'The seam between the two stopped existing.' is structurally commanding — strong, direct, past-tense closure. For the Infinex character, this line should carry some quality of mapping-toward rather than arriving-at. The character has already taken the decision; the line should point the reader forward into the space the decision opens, not cap the announcement.

The rail list 'Wire, ACH, SEPA, PIX, SPEI.' is technically correct and correctly ordered, but the full-stop terminus after SPEI seals it. There is no connective gesture linking the list to the reader's situation. The Penetrating aspect requires some sentence-motion that enters the reader's frame rather than cataloguing from outside it.

No warmth, relational weight, or vision-lift anywhere in the four units. The Spell drive requires at least one gesture where the writing inhabits the space it describes rather than reporting on it from outside.
notes_for_actor: DRIVE AXIS CORRECTION — You are running Doing (Weight + Space + Time). Infinex lives on Spell → Vision (Space + Flow + Weight). Practically: one sentence in this piece needs to release Flow. That means: do not end it with a full stop that seals the unit. Let it open toward something. The easiest candidate is the final line — instead of 'The seam between the two stopped existing.' try a form that acknowledges the space on the other side of that seam. The wall dissolved; what is now reachable?

ASPECT CORRECTION — The Penetrating aspect (Attending / Space-emphasis) is absent. You have Direct Space (single aimed lines) but no Penetrating motion — no sentence enters the reader's frame and offers them a pathway. The rail list is a catalogue seen from outside the reader's situation. Rewrite one sentence so it arrives from inside their experience: not 'here are the rails' but 'whatever channel you already use to move money — it works.'

BOUND FLOW CORRECTION — Every sentence is sealed with a full stop and does not connect to the next. Bound flow creates containment, but this character's Spell drive needs some overflow. Consider removing one full stop and replacing it with a dash or a sentence that runs into the next thought — just once, to let the mapping quality breathe.

COMMANDING TEMPO IS ON-PALETTE — Do not abandon the Weight and Direct Space; they are correct for this beat. The correction is not to soften the prose. It is to add the missing Flexible or Free gesture underneath the Strong/Direct frame, so the Drive reads as Spell-in-commanding-expression rather than pure Doing.

FINAL LINE — 'The seam between the two stopped existing.' is the strongest line in the piece and correctly identifies the lining. It is sealed too tight. The character has already taken the decision; he is not announcing an event, he is mapping a new landscape. One revision direction: let the final line face outward — toward what this now makes possible — rather than closing back over the seam itself.

old_audit.placement_read: Strong + Direct + Sustained/Bound. The copy reads as Stable inner attitude with commanding tempo — four Motion Factors present (Weight Strong, Space Direct, Time Sustained, Flow Bound), which pushes this into Drive territory rather than a three-factor Action. The four-factor activation — all factors present with equal legibility — reads as Doing Drive (Weight + Space + Time active; Flow the subdued fourth). The thesis is institutional, pressure is inward and contained, the path is undeviating. No warmth, no relational opening, no irradiation. The wall metaphor is handled as architecture, not as revelation.

infinex_fit.reason: Commanding sits within the Infinex allowed palette (primary allowed tempi). The problem is not the tempo — it is the Drive read. Commanding is legal when it carries Spell-drive undercurrent: the map-maker who has already decided. Here the copy reads Doing Drive: four factors equally activated, institutional, closed-flow, no Spell residue. Spell Drive requires Flow as the animating undercurrent — a sense that the borders of the claim are slightly larger than the stated claim. This copy seals its claim completely. The closing line ('the wall is the part that's missing') is announced, not discovered. The reader is not allowed to feel the wall dissolve; they are told the wall is missing. That is Doing, not Spell. Flow is Bound throughout, suppressing the Spell signature entirely. Additionally: the copy has no relational warmth, no Near proximity, no irradiant lift — the Infinex character is a banker-turned-trailblazer who has found the better way and now maps it for the reader. This copy maps but does not invite the reader into the territory. The super-objective ('take responsibility for the tech so the user only has to want') requires the reader to feel arrival, not receive an announcement.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: commanding with sombre lining — the copy is closest to sombre (Strong + Bound, Adream outer, slow wall-coming-down thesis) as a secondary tempo, but the Space is too Direct for sombre. The nearest legal correction is: release some Bound flow into Free on the closing line, allowing the 'wall dissolving' to feel like irradiance rather than demolition — shifting toward commanding/irradiant hybrid, which is legal Spell territory.

factual_issues: The copy says 'A FPS sort code' — FPS (Faster Payments Service, GBP) does not use a sort code in the same structural sense as a routing number or IBAN. The deployed fact says 'GBP (FPS, beta)'. FPS uses sort code + account number, but the copy presents it as a single item parallel to IBAN, CLABE, PIX code — this is defensible shorthand but risks reader confusion. The fact contract marks FPS as beta; the copy does not signal beta status. Minor factual compression, not a false claim, but worth flagging.
The copy does not name Bridge.xyz, consistent with fact receipt 'not_said' reasoning. No factual violation there.
KYC precondition is not surfaced — consistent with fact receipt. No violation.
The copy does not mention COP / Bre-B from the deployed rails list. This is an omission, not a false claim. No violation.
voice_issues: The closing line reads as announcement, not revelation. 'The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing' tells the reader what to conclude rather than letting the prior three units produce the conclusion in the reader's body. This converts Spell into Doing.

The list (IBAN / CLABE / PIX / FPS) accumulates weight downward but offers no spatial variety — each item has identical rhythmic weight. The result is institutional enumeration, not recognition. A reader who doesn't already know CLABE or PIX slides past without registration.

No Near proximity anywhere — the copy is entirely in the third-person/neutral zone ('your account', 'the bank', 'the wallet'). The Infinex character is allowed warmth and relational charge. This copy reads like a product specification issued by an institution.

'Wire it the way you'd wire a bank' is the one moment of Near contact — it addresses the reader directly in a familiar imperative. It is the strongest line in the copy because it has Light + Quick contact (cool tempo within the larger commanding arc). This single gesture is doing more relational work than everything else combined. The Actor should study why that line feels different and replicate its motion quality in the final beat.
notes_for_actor: The closing line must release, not announce. You have built sustained pressure across four units — when you arrive at 'the wall is the part that's missing,' the reader should feel the wall go, not be told it has gone. Replace the declarative seal with a line that opens slightly — let the phrase lean outward rather than closing. The motion correction is: release Bound flow into Free on the final beat. One structural option: make the final line a half-question or a spatial gesture toward the reader's own account, not a concluded thesis.

The list section is too uniform. IBAN and CLABE and PIX are not the same weight in the reader's body — some are familiar (routing number), some are foreign. Give one of the unfamiliar rail-names a small parenthetical context (not a definition — a touch of proximity) so the reader's body registers recognition rather than slides. This is a Near micro-gesture, not an explanation. Motion correction: one Dabbing contact within the column.

'Wire it the way you'd wire a bank' is your best line — it is the one place where Light + Quick contact activates Near proximity. The final paragraph needs the same quality of contact. Right now the closing paragraph has no Near in it at all — it is all map and no warmth. Motion correction: find one word in the final two lines that names the reader's experience rather than the system's architecture.

The Drive is reading Doing because all four factors are equally sealed. To restore Spell signature: deliberately leave one factor slightly open — either let Flow release slightly (Free tendency in one clause) or allow Space to flex briefly (one indirect gesture that acknowledges the reader's prior expectation before dissolving it). The actor's table work identified Spell as the primary drive; the prose must carry a residue of larger-than-stated — something implied beyond what is claimed. Right now the copy claims exactly what it demonstrates, no more. Spell requires the claim to feel like the edge of a larger territory.

The through-action is 'to reveal that the bank wall just dissolved inside one account.' The word 'reveal' requires a movement toward discovery, not delivery. Check each sentence: is it handing something to the reader (Near/Spell) or informing them of a fact (Doing/Stable commanding)? The copy is currently all delivery. Shift at least the final unit to a reveal posture — something the reader discovers rather than receives.

old_audit.placement_read: Stable attitude, Light + Direct, Gliding → Dabbing. The prose functions informationally — it delivers a state-change fact, orients the reader via analogy, and closes. No relational warmth, no vision-lift, no weight of conviction. This is Doing Drive territory: Weight present as Light (not subdued), Space and Time active, Flow subdued as containment rather than Spell's characteristic slow-unfold. The copy reads as a confident functional notice — the account can do this now, here is how it resolves — without the penetrating or irradiating quality Infinex requires.

infinex_fit.reason: self-contained is a beat-only/reserve tempo for Infinex, not a primary. It appears in the allowed palette but is designated reserve — it cannot carry a release card on its own. More critically, the through-action is 'to reveal that the bank wall just dissolved inside one account.' The current prose does not perform dissolution; it performs notification. The Lining (bank-vs-wallet wall stopped existing) is absent from the surface. The reader receives a feature update, not a structural-change reveal. Infinex primary tempi require commanding, practical, sombre, irradiant, or sociable — all of which carry more weight (Strong) or more relational warmth/lift than self-contained delivers. The copy stays too flat and too closed (Bound flow, no release) to execute the through-action.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: irradiant — Light + Free, Floating → Flicking, Adream outer. Would preserve the lightness already present but open the flow to release, add the buoyant forward-warmth that marks a genuine structural shift rather than a feature note. Alternatively, sombre — Strong + Bound, Adream outer — would perform the weight of the dissolution ('the wall came down') with held gravity, if the Actor wants to land the magnitude rather than the lift.

voice_issues: The through-action is dissolution of the bank/wallet wall. The prose delivers a feature availability notice. These are not the same action. 'Can now receive fiat the way a bank account does' is orientation by analogy — it tells the reader what category to put this in. It does not perform the structural claim that the wall stopped existing. The Lining is not visible in the surface.

The Bound flow closes the copy before the reader can feel the arrival. 'It lands as USDC, on-chain.' is a fact-and-stop. The dissolution event has no release moment — the copy contains what should overflow.

Two declaratives at equal weight give the copy a bulletin shape. A bulletin is self-contained. The release card calls for something with more dimension — either the weight of magnitude (sombre) or the lift of arrival (irradiant).
notes_for_actor: The analogy ('the way a bank account does') is doing good work orienting the reader — keep the navigation. The problem is it orients without moving. Let the analogy do its job and then let something shift. The wall has to come down inside the sentence, not just be described.

Choose a direction before rewriting: (a) irradiant — add Free flow, let the arrival open outward rather than close. The USDC landing is not a contained fact; it is something arriving that keeps arriving, permanently. Write from that permanence and let it breathe. (b) sombre — add Strong weight, let the bank-wall dissolution land with gravity. The reader has been told for years these are separate systems. Acknowledge the weight of that history dissolving. One of these is the tempo; pick it before touching the copy.

If irradiant: the closing sentence needs to open, not seal. 'It lands as USDC, on-chain.' shuts. Find the sentence that lets the USDC arrival carry the sense of permanent structural change — something that radiates forward rather than stops.

If sombre: the opening sentence needs weight, not just analogy. 'The way a bank account does' is Light and orienting. For sombre, the sentence needs to press — the bank-account comparison becomes an acknowledgment of what used to be impossible, now done.

The 'now' in 'can now receive' is the only temporal marker of change. It is bearing too much load. The structural shift needs to be enacted in the movement of the prose, not stored in a single adverb.
Bound flow note: the comma before 'on-chain' is a small habit of containment. In irradiant, drop the comma or restructure — let the landing arrive without a gate on it.

old_audit.placement_read: Strong + Flexible + Sustained + Bound. Motor: Wringing toward an unreleased Slash. Drive read is Doing (Weight + Space + Time active; Flow subdued/bound rather than absent). Spell would require Flow to be the stressed factor; here Flow is the control valve, not the motor. The piece is working through practical complexity toward a structural revelation — that is Doing drive in Stable/practical register with a Wring motor.

infinex_fit.reason: Primary tempo reads as practical (Stable; Strong + Flexible; Wringing → Slashing). Practical is in the allowed Infinex palette. However, the Flow pole is Bound rather than Free, and the drive reads as Doing (Weight + Space + Time) rather than Spell or Vision. Infinex placement spec requires stable + penetrating + flow on the drive axis (Spell → Vision). The Bound Flow here suppresses rather than activates the Flow factor — it does not register as Spell. The piece is doing Doing-drive work: solving a structural problem (bank wall), naming the parts, stating the answer. That is useful but it is not the Infinex character's register. Infinex holds the map already made; it does not problem-solve in public. The through-action ('reveal that the bank wall just dissolved') is correct in intent but the execution re-erects the wall by enumerating the rails and currencies first — the piece is doing the dissolving in real time rather than standing after dissolution and pointing. This activates Doing drive where Spell drive is specified.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: practical — allowed but currently executing in Doing-drive rather than Spell-drive. The fix is not to change tempo; it is to shift the drive axis. Practical tempo in Spell drive would carry the same Strong + Flexible but with Flow as the mobile factor rather than the containment vessel.

factual_issues: No deployed-fact violations detected. Rails enumerated (ACH, SEPA, SPEI, PIX, FPS, Bre-B) match the fact contract exactly. Currencies (USD, EUR, MXN, BRL, GBP, COP) match. Settlement output (USDC, on-chain) matches. KYC precondition, Bridge identity, and build-state correctly omitted per not-said list.
voice_issues: The piece reconstructs the problem (bank/wallet separation) and then dissolves it — but Infinex does not reconstruct problems. Infinex stands after the decision and maps. The two-list opening performs the infrastructure complexity rather than speaking from the other side of it. This is a Doing-drive construction: here is the complex thing, here is how it resolves. Spell-drive construction would open already resolved: the wall is gone; here is what that means for your salary.

The thesis sentence ('The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing') is the strongest line and arrives last. In Spell-drive practical tempo, the weight-bearing thesis arrives first or early and the subsequent material radiates from it rather than building toward it. The current structure is inductive (enumerate → conclude); Infinex reads deductive (land → detail).

Bound Flow throughout: the piece is more contained than the character. Infinex's Spell-drive register has a degree of Flow activation — not Free overflow, but a quality of quiet irradiance or held conviction that reaches the reader. The current piece is technically correct and controlled but does not reach. It reports; it does not emanate.
notes_for_actor: Move the thesis to the opening. 'The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing' is your strongest sentence and your actual through-action. Lead with it. The lists then become evidence radiating from a settled claim rather than runup to a reveal. This shifts the structure from Doing-drive (problem → solution) to Spell-drive (position → demonstration).

Trim one of the two lists or collapse them into the same sentence. Two separate enumerations of essentially the same claim (rails, then currencies) activate Flexible pathing without Spell-drive payoff — they feel like complexity-management rather than cartography. Infinex already holds the map; it does not demonstrate the map was hard to draw.

Release the Flow valve on the thesis sentence. The current version holds: 'The wall between the bank and the wallet is the part that's missing.' One small kinetic unlock — e.g. 'The wall between the bank and the wallet is gone' or a version that doesn't end on an absent thing — lets the Wring motor complete into a Slash. The current ending ('the part that's missing') names absence; Spell-drive names presence from the other side of absence.

Do not add warmth, exclamation, or personality. The problem is not that the piece is too cold — practical is a legal tempo. The problem is structural drive. Keep the weight, keep the control, keep the Flexible routing through multiple rails. Just land from inside the resolved position rather than resolving in public.

Check the opening line for reader-address mode. 'Whatever rail your salary uses' is Flexible and reaches toward the reader — this is good. But 'uses' puts the reader in present tense still inside the old system. If the wall is already gone, the opening might land them inside the new condition rather than naming their old one. Consider whether the entry should meet them in transit or already arrived.

old_audit.placement_read: The sentence reads as Stable|Penetrating character — single-channel, weighted, held — with Flow stress (Bound), which is legal for Stable. Drive table cell stable|penetrating|flow lands at primary=spell, secondary=doing, introvert=passion, extravert=vision. The visible surface here is doing: the sentence demonstrates a completed action ('takes a wire... the way a bank does') and foregrounds functional capability rather than the mapped Vision endpoint or the resting Spell. The extravert slot should be Vision (the wider picture, the dissolved wall); instead the line delivers a working-action proof. That is doing as the visible projection, not vision. The lining (the bank wall just stopped existing) is not surfaced at all.

infinex_fit.reason: Pressing/Bound is legal motor and flow stress for Stable|Penetrating. The tempo (commanding) is on the primary allowed palette. The failure is drive-slot: the sentence's visible projection is doing — it reads as functional demonstration ('here is what it can do, proved'). For Infinex|Penetrating|Flow the extravert slot must be Vision: the reader should glimpse the wider mapped territory (the wall that no longer exists, the world where this is unremarkable) rather than receive a capability proof. Doing belongs in the secondary slot, not foregrounded as the surface. Additionally, the lining ('the bank-vs-wallet wall just stopped existing') is entirely absent from the prose — the Outer Action is present but the hidden Inner Action is unreadable, leaving the line with no through-action depth.
infinex_fit.nearest_allowed_read: Retain the Pressing motor and the single declarative stance (Direct/Sustained/Strong). Shift the visible surface from doing (capability proof) to vision (territory claim): instead of demonstrating what the account can now do, let the line name what has changed about the world the user inhabits. The bank wall's disappearance — not the wire deposit feature — is the Vision endpoint. Doing can remain as the structural spine; it just cannot be the face.

factual_issues: observation: 'takes a wire' — USD wire is one of the supported Bridge.xyz rails (ACH/wire confirmed in deployed_facts). The claim is factually grounded. However, the sentence uses 'wire' as the single example, which is technically accurate but not exhaustive; this is a deliberate editorial choice (per release card: rails list is 'not the point') and does not constitute a factual error.

observation: 'the way a bank does' — Bridge virtual accounts receive fiat to a permanent account+routing number (USD) or IBAN (EUR) etc., then auto-convert and settle on-chain. The mechanical analogy to a bank account receiving a wire is accurate at the user-facing level.
